# terrible_purestorage_flashblade_purefb_certs (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `certificate` (String) Required for *import* A valid signed certicate in PEM format (Base64 encoded) Includes the "-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----" and "-----END CERTIFICATE-----" lines Does not exceed 3000 characters in length
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `common_name` (String) The fully qualified domain name (FQDN) of the current system For example, the common name for https://pureblade.example.com is pureblade.example.com, or *.example.com for a wildcard certificate This can also be the management IP address of the system or the shortname of the current system. Maximum of 64 characters If not provided this will default to the shortname of the system
- `country` (String) The two-letter ISO code for the country where your organization is located
- `days` (Number) The number of valid days for the self-signed certificate being generated If not specified, the self-signed certificate expires after 3650 days.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `email` (String) The email address used to contact your organization
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `export_file` (String) Name of file to contain Certificate Signing Request when `status sign` Name of file to export the current SSL Certificate when `status export` File will be overwritten if it already exists
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `generate` (Boolean) Generate a new private key. If not selected, the certificate will use the existing key
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `intermediate_cert` (String) Intermeadiate certificate provided by the CA
- `key` (String) If the Certificate Signed Request (CSR) was not constructed on the system or the private key has changed since construction the CSR, provide a new private key here
- `key_algorithm` (String) The key algorithm used to generate the certificate. This field can only be specified when creating a new self-signed certificate
- `key_size` (Number) The key size in bits if you generate a new private key
- `locality` (String) The full name of the city where your organization is located
- `name` (String) Name of the SSL Certificate
- `org_unit` (String) The department within your organization that is managing the certificate
- `organization` (String) The full and exact legal name of your organization. The organization name should not be abbreviated and should include suffixes such as Inc, Corp, or LLC.
- `passphrase` (String) Passphrase if the private key is encrypted
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `province` (String) The full name of the state or province where your organization is located
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) Action for the module to perform *present* will create or re-create an SSL certificate *absent* will delete an existing SSL certificate *sign* will construct a Certificate Signing request (CSR) *export* will export the exisitng SSL certificate *import* will import a CA provided certificate.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
